name: The Founding Years
description: F1 is established with front-engined grand prix cars derived from pre-war designs. The era was dominated by legendary drivers like Juan Manuel Fangio driving legendary machinery.

name: Rear-Engine Revolution
description: Cooper pioneers the rear-engine layout, making front-engined cars obsolete almost overnight. Lotus introduces the monocoque chassis, making cars much lighter and stiffer.

name: Cosworth DFV & Wings
description: Inverted wings are introduced to produce aerodynamic downforce, dramatically increasing cornering speeds. The Ford-Cosworth DFV V8 engine becomes the standard power unit for most teams on the grid.

name: Ground Effect & Turbo Era
description: Teams exploit under-car Venturi tunnels to pull cars to the track using ground effects. High-boost turbochargers push engine outputs past 1,000 horsepower in qualifying trims.

name: The Golden V10 Era
description: Naturally aspirated 3.5L and later 3.0L V10 engines produce legendary high-pitched screams and extreme power curves. Electronic driver aids like traction control and active suspension are briefly introduced and then restricted.

name: The High-RPM V8 Era
description: Formula 1 mandates 2.4L V8 engines limited to high RPMs to reduce costs and speeds. Kinetic energy recovery systems (KERS) and drag reduction systems (DRS) make their debut.

name: V6 Turbo Hybrid Era
description: The sport introduces 1.6L turbocharged hybrid power units with sophisticated heat and kinetic energy recovery systems. Mercedes dominates the early years of this highly efficient, high-torque era.

name: Ground Effect Revival
description: The regulations are overhauled to bring back ground effect aerodynamics to improve close racing and overtaking. Over-wheel winglets and simplified front wings give cars a futuristic look.
